{{Infobox character
| name = Malachi
| introduced = House of Blades
| name = Malachi
| aliases = Malachi Daiasus, Overlord Malachi
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = Overlord, Traveler of Naraka
| abilities = summon a thousand orbs of screaming orange flame, summon ants the size of wolfhound that blaze with flame
| affiliations = Damascan Kingdom
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Malachi Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Malachi Daiasus, also known as Overlord Malachi, was a high-ranking official serving the [[Damascan Kingdom]] under [[King Zakareth VI]]. As an Overlord, he managed a portion of [[Damasca]] and was responsible for overseeing annual human sacrifices, such as the raid on the village of [[Myria]]. While some stories characterized him as a distant and just leader rather than a tyrant, his actions—including the collection of sacrifices—led to significant conflict. He was known for his vanity, often wearing specific colors like purple and brown, and possessed a throne made of amethyst and dark wood. Malachi was a [[Traveler]] of [[Naraka]], a realm associated with law and order. During his final confrontation, he utilized a powerful mask to attempt a summons of the [[Arbiter]] Vedreith, an act that caused him immense physical agony and allowed him to draw vast amounts of power from his [[Territory]]. He ultimately died during a battle with [[Alin]], an event celebrated by some as the defeat of a tyrant. Following his death, his wife [[Adrienne]], who was not a [[Traveler]], took over the management of his realm.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Malachi Daiasus is an Overlord who serves as a faithful slave to [[King Zakareth the Sixth]], managing a corner of the [[Damascan Kingdom]] that includes [[Myria village]]. Known as a distant but just ruler, this vain man wears the [[Ragnarus mask]] and specific colors, often sporting a crimson half-mask that sticks to his face. His authority is enforced by soldiers and officers who act as raiders, dragging people away and taking captives like [[Leah]] from their villages. Malachi is responsible for the annual sacrifice, a process involving nine people, and possesses the power to summon flaming orange orbs and wolfhound-sized fire ants. Despite his status, he bears a [[Naraka brand]] as a shining red mark on his palm. His reign ended on midsummer's eve when he was killed by [[Alin]], leaving behind a legacy responsible for the deaths of at least a dozen Myrian villagers.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Overlord [[Malachi]] has official soldiers who are raiders.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2">''House of Blades'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]]'s soldiers attacked the village gate.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is an Overlord.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is a kingdom.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Overlord [[Malachi]] manages a corner of [[Damasca]] in the name of the King.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c3">''House of Blades'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Overlord [[Malachi]]'s reputation is that he is distant but just.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Overlord [[Malachi]] is a ruler of a realm containing [[Myria village]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The prisoners are on their way to [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Overlord [[Malachi]] has taken people from [[Simon]]'s village.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c5">''House of Blades'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Overlord [[Malachi]] has [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Overlord [[Malachi]] is responsible for this year's sacrifice.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c10">''House of Blades'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is an Overlord whom the children's father works for as a free merchant.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11">''House of Blades'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] has soldiers that drag people away.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13">''House of Blades'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is a vain man who wears specific colors.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is an Overlord who has a [[Naraka brand]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14">''House of Blades'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]]'s full name is [[Malachi]] Daiasus.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is holding [[Leah]] and other captives.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is caring for at least one Myrian captive at his own personal expense.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] has an officer and soldiers.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] Daiasus is an Overlord in service to the [[Damascan Kingdom]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c19">''House of Blades'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] Daiasus is a faithful slave of [[King Zakareth the Sixth]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] has a shining red mark on the palm of his hand.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] claims that the sacrifice involves only nine people a year from the whole kingdom.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20">''House of Blades'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] uses a red half-mask that sticks to his face.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] can summon a thousand orbs of screaming orange flame.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] can summon ants the size of wolfhounds that blaze with flame.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] wears the [[Ragnarus mask]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is an Overlord who wears a crimson mask covering half of his face.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] has a branded hand.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] ordered [[Leah]] to be taken from her village.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] has a red Gate.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] wears a half-mask.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is dead, killed by [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] is the lord over the lands where [[Simon]] is located.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c22">''House of Blades'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] Daiasus was responsible for the deaths of at least a dozen Myrian villagers and is dead.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c23">''House of Blades'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]] died on midsummer's eve.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c23" />}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|An Overlord and a [[Traveler]] of [[Naraka]], Malachi once granted [[Erastes]] permission to retire while utilizing a mask to summon immense power from his [[Territory]]. Following a raid on [[Myria]], he held [[Leah]] captive in his tower for several weeks, though she later claimed she was never in any actual danger. Malachi eventually engaged in combat with [[Simon]], who played a role in his defeat at the hands of [[Alin]]. The Overlord died one month prior to the council, an event so significant that [[Alin]] threatened to inflict a similar [[fate]] upon [[Grandmaster Naraka]] should his sisters be harmed.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Malachi]] gave [[Erastes]] permission to retire.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Overlord [[Malachi]] wore a mask that allowed him to call terrifying amounts of power from his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Malachi]] is dead.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Overlord [[Malachi]] was defeated by [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Malachi]] conducted a raid on [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] rescued [[Leah]] from [[Malachi]]’s tower.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] was left in Overlord [[Malachi]]'s hands for weeks.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Malachi]] was an Overlord and a [[Traveler]] of [[Naraka]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Overlord [[Malachi]] died one month prior to the council.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Malachi]] fought with [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] played a role in the death of Overlord [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] claims she was never in danger from [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] threatens that if anything happens to his sisters, he will make [[Grandmaster Naraka]] suffer the same [[fate]] as Overlord [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Malachi]] is an Overlord with a home in [[Bel Calem]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5">''City of Light'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Malachi]] used the shattered mask for years.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“My apologies, Highness, but I am here on the orders of Overlord Malachi.”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"Another Traveler, from Overlord Malachi."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"Overlord Malachi Daiasus," Leah said coldly, as she did she was proud to hear all the authority of Damascan royalty in her voice.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"Malachi is caring for at least one of the Myrian captives at his own personal expense," Ezera added.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“You were the one who killed the Overlord.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
